task:
Ticket 0083, atom 1 of a multi-atom plan: add a governed Oz-chat affordance to SET retention settings through the daemon/settings spine, so the solo founder never hand-edits local/settings.json. This atom covers ONLY the settings-write command; driving + surfacing the observed GC pass is a later atom — do not build that here.

WHAT TO CHANGE (follow the existing oz-chat command pattern exactly — study how `refresh`/`stop` flow from parseOzCommand -> OzCommand union -> executeOzCommand handler -> injected OzChatOps op -> reply/action):

1. packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts:
   - Add a new OzCommand variant: `{ readonly kind: 'retention'; readonly enabled: boolean; readonly keepLastNPerWorkspace?: number }`.
   - parseOzCommand: parse `retention enable [N]` (enabled=true, optional positive-integer N -> keepLastNPerWorkspace) and `retention disable` (enabled=false). On a malformed N or missing subcommand, return the `unknown` command with a helpful hint, matching how other commands handle bad args.
   - Add `'retention'` to the OzChatAction.type union.
   - executeOzCommand: handle kind 'retention' by calling a new injected op (see #3). Return an OzChatReply with command:'retention', ok:true, and a plain-English reply stating the new state, e.g. "Retention enabled, keeping the last 25 runs per workspace." / "Retention disabled." Include an action of type 'retention' carrying the resulting settings if that matches the surrounding action shape.
   - Add `retention enable [N] | retention disable` to HELP_HINT.

2. packages/daemon/src/settings.ts: reuse the existing `mergeWriteSettings` / `sanePatch` / `resolveRetentionConfig` — do NOT add a parallel settings writer. The op must patch ONLY the `retention` field via the existing atomic merge-write path. Do NOT change DEFAULT_SETTINGS (retention must stay disabled by default).

3. Add the op alongside the other launcher ops (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, the same place requestDaemonRestart lives) e.g. `setRetention(ctx, { enabled, keepLastNPerWorkspace })`: it calls mergeWriteSettings to persist the retention patch, appends an audit entry (reuse appendAudit; action e.g. 'retention-settings' with the resulting enabled + keepLastNPerWorkspace) so founder intent is visible in the governed audit log, and returns the resolved retention config. Wire it through OzChatOps the same way restartDaemon is wired.

4. Tests (mirror the existing daemon/tests/settings.test.ts and the oz-chat parse/execute tests):
   - parseOzCommand: `retention enable`, `retention enable 10`, `retention disable`, and a malformed case map to the right OzCommand.
   - executeOzCommand `retention enable 10`: settings.json now has retention {enabled:true, keepLastNPerWorkspace:10}, a 'retention-settings' audit entry was appended, and the reply text reflects the state. A `retention disable` case flips enabled to false.

ACCEPTANCE: `pnpm -w typecheck` passes; the daemon package test suite passes (`pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` or the repo's equivalent) including your new cases. The command persists via the existing mergeWriteSettings atomic write and audit-wraps the intent.

MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE: do not edit local/settings.json (it is the gitignored machine-local cache). Do not change DEFAULT_SETTINGS. Do not touch retention-gc / runRetentionGcOnce / the boot GC path or observe-retention-live.mjs — that is a later atom. Do not add a second settings-writing code path. Keep the diff to oz-chat.ts, launcher.ts (op + OzChatOps wiring), and tests; touch settings.ts only if a tiny export is needed.

Report the exact files changed, the new command syntax, and paste the test command output (exit code + pass counts).

Ticket 0083, atom 2: make the governed `retention enable` affordance DRIVE and SURFACE the observed GC pass, so the solo founder sees the result in Oz without running observe-retention-live.mjs or any terminal command. Atom 1 already landed the settings-write command + audit. Build on it.

DESIGN (already decided — on-demand, one founder action): enabling retention via the Oz-chat command runs the GC pass immediately using the SAME function the daemon boot uses, and the chat reply surfaces the observed result. Disabling just flips the flag (no GC).

WHAT TO CHANGE:

1. Single-owner footprint helper. Add ONE TypeScript owner for the `local/` footprint + protected-run measurement that the founder affordance reports — e.g. `measureLocalFootprint(localRoot)` returning the same contract observe-retention-live.mjs already measures: localBytes, dbBytes, walBytes, auditBytes, and run-dir counts. Put it where the daemon/core can import it (packages/core/src/runner/ or packages/daemon/src/). Do NOT introduce a second copy of this contract — the daemon command must import this one owner, not re-implement footprint math inline.

2. Drive the pass in setRetention (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts). When enabling (enabled:true): capture the before footprint via the helper, call the EXISTING `runRetentionGcOnce(ctx)` (the exact function server.ts boot calls — it emits the `retention-gc` audit entry and protects in-flight runs via protectedRunIds; do NOT fork a parallel GC path), capture the after footprint, and return a LaunchResult body carrying: retention {enabled, keepLastNPerWorkspace}, footprintBefore, footprintAfter, and the runRetentionGcOnce result fields (prunedRunIds, skippedProtectedRunIds, storeRunRowsKept, dirsRemoved, failures). On disable (enabled:false): only persist + audit the flag flip as today; do NOT run GC.

3. Surface it (packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts retentionReply). On enable, the plain-English reply must state: retention enabled + keep-N; the before→after `local/` footprint (e.g. bytes freed); which runs were pruned (or 'none'); and explicitly that protected/in-flight runs were skipped and NOT pruned (name the skippedProtectedRunIds, or 'no protected run pruned' when empty); and that a retention-gc audit entry was written. Keep the disable reply as-is ('Retention disabled.'). Extend the OzChatAction 'retention' payload as needed to carry the observed result for the UI.

4. Reuse observe-retention-live.mjs measurements, do NOT duplicate them. If the .mjs encodes the same footprint/protected-run contract, prefer making the new TS helper the single owner and reducing the script to consume it. If the script's standalone node:sqlite/.mjs runtime genuinely cannot import the TS owner without a build step, do NOT fork a divergent second contract — leave the script unchanged and state in your report exactly why, so verify can judge the one-owner tradeoff.

5. Tests (packages/daemon/tests): (a) `retention enable` with prunable terminal runs present runs the GC pass, the reply reports a non-empty footprint delta and the pruned run ids, and a `retention-gc` audit entry exists afterward; (b) a protected/in-flight run (status running or in ctx.inFlight) is NOT pruned and the reply names it as skipped; (c) `retention disable` writes the flag and does NOT run GC (no new retention-gc audit entry, no pruning). Mirror the existing runRetentionGcOnce tests in mutations.test.ts for fixture setup.

ACCEPTANCE: `pnpm -w typecheck` passes; `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` passes including the new cases; `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` passes if you add a core helper. The founder can type `retention enable` in Oz chat and see the observed GC pass (footprint delta + pruned + protected-skipped + audit entry) with no terminal command and no settings.json hand-edit.

MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE: reuse runRetentionGcOnce — do not write a second GC path. One owner for footprint measurement — do not duplicate the contract. Do not edit local/settings.json. Do not change DEFAULT_SETTINGS. Do not touch the wrap-up Play or governance docs (that is atom 3). Keep the diff to the footprint helper, launcher.ts, oz-chat.ts, optionally observe-retention-live.mjs, and tests.

Report exact files changed, where the single footprint owner lives, whether observe-retention-live.mjs was rewired or left standalone (and why), and paste the typecheck + test output (exit codes + pass counts).

Ticket 0083, atom 3 (final): close acceptance bullet 3 — wrap-up closeouts for any settings or lifecycle change must emit a GOVERNED founder action, never a founder-executed terminal command or a hand-edit of machine-local config. Atoms 1-2 already shipped the governed `retention enable` affordance; this atom adds the durable closeout guardrail so the run_279 leak (a settings.json hand-edit + node commands handed to the founder) cannot recur.

OWNER MAP FIRST (do this before editing): the closeout-emission contract is owned by the base wrap-up Play at `packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md`. Grep for a SYNCED TWIN: `grep -rl 'Wrap-up Play' templates/ packages/` — if a byte-synced copy exists under templates/** (or elsewhere), it is a second owner and you MUST update it identically. Also read `packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts` and `packages/core/tests/plays.test.ts` to learn what they pin (byte-sync between base and template, portability/ADR-0012, section structure) and keep them green.

WHAT TO CHANGE in the wrap-up Play (and any synced twin): in the closeout sections (the `Next Action` rule around line 37 / 'Run Status' guidance / 'Founder Decision Needed' / 'Recommended Next Step'), add a ROLE-NEUTRAL rule, in this spirit: any settings or lifecycle change the founder must make has to be expressed as a governed founder action — a chat command, a UI affordance, or a Play backed by the daemon commit/lifecycle spine — never a founder-run terminal/shell/`node` command and never a hand-edit of machine-local internal config. CRITICAL distinction to preserve: the existing F18 allowance that a one-command proof HARNESS (e.g. `node scripts/<proof>.mjs`) is an acceptable RUNNABLE *verification* the founder runs stays valid — but it is NOT an acceptable way to *apply* a settings or lifecycle change; applying such a change must route through a governed affordance. Make the rule sharp enough that 'add `retention.enabled` to settings.json and run these node commands' is unambiguously forbidden as a Next Action, while 'offer to craft `node scripts/proof.mjs`' remains allowed.

PORTABILITY (ADR-0012, mandatory): the wrap-up Play is BASE governance shipped to every workspace. The new rule must contain NO product-specific nouns — no 'retention', no 'settings.json', no cocoder paths. Phrase it generically (settings/lifecycle change, machine-local config, governed affordance). If you cannot state it portably, stop and say so.

DOCS (only if one already exists): if there is an existing docs/** page documenting retention enablement or the observe-retention-live workflow, update it to point the founder at the governed `retention enable` / `retention disable` Oz-chat command instead of any settings.json hand-edit. Do NOT create a new doc if none exists; do not touch cocoder/** (outside your scope).

ACCEPTANCE: `pnpm -w typecheck` passes; `pnpm --filter @cocoder/personas test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` pass (specifically base-personas.test.ts and plays.test.ts), with base and any template twin kept in sync. The guardrail is present, portable, and distinguishes a proof harness from applying a settings/lifecycle change.

MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE: keep the existing F18 RUNNABLE Next Action guidance intact — you are ADDING a settings/lifecycle carve-out, not replacing it. Do not introduce product-specific nouns into base. Do not edit cocoder/** (failure catalog / tickets are Oscar's lane). Do not re-touch oz-chat.ts/launcher.ts/local-footprint.ts. Keep base/template byte-sync if the test requires it.

Report: exact files changed, whether a template twin existed and was synced, the portable wording you added, and paste the typecheck + persona/core test output (exit codes + pass counts).
